title: Convert min Heap to max Heap

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array numsnumsnums that represents a **min-heap** (in array form). Your task is to **convert this min-heap into a max-heap** and output the resulting array representation.

A **min-heap** is a complete binary tree in which the value at each node is **smaller than or equal to** the values of its children.

A **max-heap** is a complete binary tree in which the value at each node is **greater than or equal to** the values of its children.

You need to rearrange the elements of the given min-heap array so that it satisfies the **max-heap property**.

If multiple valid max-heaps are possible, any correct one will be accepted.

Function DeclarationFunction NameconvertMinToMaxHeapconvertMinToMaxHeapconvertMinToMaxHeap – This function converts a given **min-heap** (array representation) into a **max-heap** by rearranging its elements in-place.

Parameters
numsnumsnums : An integer array representing a valid min-heap.
Return Value
This function does **not** return anything.
It modifies the input array numsnumsnums in-place so that it satisfies the **max-heap** property.
constraints: The input array always represents a valid min-heap.
The first line contains an integer TTT — the number of test cases.
The first line contains an integer nnn — the number of elements.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers representing the min-heap array.
For each test case, print the array representation of the converted **max-heap**.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, the min-heap [3, 5, 7, 9, 11] is converted into a valid max-heap [11, 9, 7, 3, 5].
In the second test case, [2, 8, 6, 15, 10, 12] becomes [15, 10, 12, 8, 2, 6]. Both arrays satisfy the max-heap property.
